name: Slope
description: How steep a line is; measures rate of change

content.overview: Slope is how steep a line is on a graph. It tells you how fast something is changing. {{viz:diagram-01}}
content.explanation:
Slope = how much Y changes when X changes 1 unit.

**The formula:** slope = (Y₂ - Y₁) / (X₂ - X₁) = rise / run

Think of it like climbing a hill. The slope tells you how steep it is.

**In physics:** {{viz:chart-01}}
- On a distance-time graph: slope = speed (how fast you're going)
- On a velocity-time graph: slope = acceleration (how fast speed is changing)
- Steep slope = fast change
- Flat slope = slow change
- Negative slope = going backwards or decreasing

**Examples:**
- A slope of 5 means: for every 1 unit right, go 5 units up
- A slope of 0 means: flat line (nothing is changing)
- A slope of -2 means: for every 1 right, go 2 down (decreasing) {{viz:anim-01}}

Slope is just a number that describes how quickly something is changing. That's it. But it's EVERYWHERE in physics.
keyPoints: Slope = rise / run = change in Y / change in X
In physics, slope often = speed or rate of change
Slope is the same everywhere on a straight line
content.whyItMatters: Slope is how we measure rates of change in physics. Speed, acceleration, all described by slope.
